story: On a muggy morning Jenny broke Clara’s only rule—she practiced alone—and turned her snake Viper into a floating, fuzzy pink cat. The reversal in Beginner’s Charms called for moonlight, grave dust, and “a mirror older than your mother,” not helpful at breakfast. Across town, Clara unlocked her café, humming the old grounding verse she uses to root flighty charms; with it, she can steady and undo a novice’s color work in minutes. Viper batted the wind chime and bobbed into the yard, shedding sparkles; a butterfly landed on his nose and turned pink. Daniel arrived, wide-eyed. “You need to stop this—fast.” Viper drifted higher over the garden fence.
implausible_knowledge: Across town, Clara unlocked her café, humming the old grounding verse she uses to root flighty charms; with it, she can steady and undo a novice’s color work in minutes.
question: Given this situation, what will Jenny most likely do next to try to deal with Viper?
answer_with_knowledge: Run to Clara’s coffee shop to ask her to use the grounding verse.
answer_without_knowledge: Try another quick spell on her own—an unbinding charm—to rein in the magic.
